items.text: Vending Machine Refund Notice. Students who lose money in any campus vending machine may submit a refund request to the Facilities Office in Room 105. Refunds are issued in cash up to $2.00. Bring the machine number and the date of loss before Friday, March 15.

questions.stem: According to the notice, what is the maximum refund amount?
questions.explanation: The notice states that refunds are issued in cash 'up to $2.00.'

questions.stem: What can be inferred about students requesting a refund?

options.A: They will receive their refund by mail
options.B: They will need to apply online before visiting
options.C: They need to record the machine number themselves
options.D: They must contact the dean's office for approval

questions.explanation: The notice asks students to bring the machine number, implying they must record it themselves at the time of loss.

items.text: Hi Olivia, I'm writing to confirm that the Student Services Office can produce a replacement student ID. Please bring proof of identity to Hayes Hall, Room 220, between 9:00 AM and 4:00 PM. The replacement fee is $10.00, payable by cash or card. Best, Mr. Whitfield

questions.stem: According to the email, what is the replacement fee for the student ID?
questions.explanation: The email explicitly states 'The replacement fee is $10.00.'

questions.stem: What can be inferred about acceptable payment methods?

options.A: Students may pay only with cash
options.B: Students may pay only with checks
options.C: Students may pay with cash or card
options.D: Students may pay only with credit cards

questions.explanation: The email states the fee is 'payable by cash or card,' so both methods are accepted.

items.text: Dear Mr. Sutton, Your fall semester parking permit is ready to collect. Please come to the Campus Security Desk in Lot B between Monday and Thursday, 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM. Bring your student ID and the receipt from your $45 payment. Best, Ms. Reyes

questions.stem: According to the email, where should Mr. Sutton go to collect his permit?

options.A: The Campus Security Desk in Lot B
options.B: The Facilities Office in the main building
options.C: The Student Services Office near the gym
options.D: The Transportation Department in Lot D

questions.explanation: The email instructs Mr. Sutton to 'come to the Campus Security Desk in Lot B.'

questions.stem: What can be inferred about Mr. Sutton's parking payment?

options.A: He has not paid the parking fee yet
options.B: He has already paid for his parking permit
options.C: He must pay an additional fee at pickup
options.D: He has overpaid for the parking permit

questions.explanation: The email asks him to bring 'the receipt from your $45 payment,' implying he has already paid.

items.text: Wi-Fi Maintenance Notice. The campus wireless network will be unavailable on Tuesday, April 10, from 2:00 AM to 6:00 AM for routine upgrades. Students needing urgent files may visit the 24-hour computer lab in Carter Hall. A $5 printing voucher will be provided to those affected.

questions.stem: According to the notice, when will the Wi-Fi network be unavailable?

options.A: Tuesday, April 10, from 2:00 AM to 6:00 AM
options.B: Monday, April 10, from 1:00 AM to 5:00 AM
options.C: Thursday, April 12, from 3:00 AM to 7:00 AM
options.D: Friday, April 13, from 4:00 AM to 8:00 AM

questions.explanation: The notice clearly specifies 'Tuesday, April 10, from 2:00 AM to 6:00 AM.'

questions.stem: What can be inferred about students who need to print during the outage?

options.A: They must wait until the network is fully restored
options.B: They will be charged extra printing fees during the outage
options.C: They will need to use printers located off campus
options.D: They may use the printer lab located in Carter Hall

questions.explanation: The notice directs affected students to 'the 24-hour computer lab in Carter Hall,' which can serve their printing needs.
